laws.title: Washington State Minimum Wage — $17.13/hr (Effective January 1, 2026)
laws.summary: Washington's statewide minimum wage increased to $17.13 per hour on January 1, 2026. Several cities set higher local rates — notably Seattle ($21.30/hr for large employers), Tukwila ($21.65/hr), Burien, Renton, and Everett. Washington adjusts its statewide minimum wage annually based on the Consumer Price Index.
compliance_actions: Update payroll to at least $17.13/hour statewide effective January 1, 2026
If you operate in Seattle, Tukwila, Burien, Renton, Everett, or Bellingham, verify the applicable local minimum wage for your employer size — local rates exceed the state minimum
Post the 2026 Washington State Minimum Wage poster in the workplace (and applicable local wage notices if in a covered city)
Plan for the next automatic annual adjustment effective January 1, 2027
laws.source: Washington State Department of Labor & Industries
